title: Overcut/Warm-Up Regime Specialist
summary: Public F1 strategy analyses stress that undercut is not universal: on low-deg, hard-warm-up tracks, staying out can beat fresh tyres for 1-2 laps.
rationale: Public F1 strategy analyses stress that undercut is not universal: on low-deg, hard-warm-up tracks, staying out can beat fresh tyres for 1-2 laps. The current best solutions are much richer on tyre-age pressure than on overcut viability, so a dedicated expert for low-energy/track-position regimes could add complementary rank signal.
implementation_hint: Train a separate model or interaction block activated by circuit/compound regime features: low-stress track prior, high track-position value, compound warm-up penalty proxy, clean-air proxy, and whether tyre age is high but LapTime_Delta is still stable. Test blended OOF predictions against the baseline only on races/compounds where overcut conditions are plausible.
expected_effect: Should improve discrimination on false positives where the baseline over-calls imminent pits on tracks where tyres take time to switch on or track position dominates.
risk: If the dataset lacks enough explicit overcut cases, this may add noise and mostly reshuffle already-correct low-probability rows.
